# Performance Analysis

The low engagement (0.26% engagement rate) stems from **weak emotional hooks and abstract messaging**. The thread promises technical security improvements but leads with vague protection language ("new layer," "conservative pricing") that doesn't create urgency or FOMO—common drivers for protocol announcements. The specificity stops at *what* happens (automatic switching) rather than *why it matters* (e.g., "prevents $X liquidation cascades" or "saves users Y% on borrowing costs"), leaving the value proposition unclear for casual viewers who scroll past the thread opener.
